issue_title: Add an RFC 3339 only mode
issue_body:
When parsing data that I know should be in RFC 3339, I'd like a strict mode that rejects non-RFC3339-compliant strings like `2018-07-12` or ``2018-07-12T14Z` or `2018-07-12T14:08:12`.

(Mostly, I just want to be sure that I don't end up with a naive datetime when I expected a timezone-aware one, but validating that my data is actually RFC 3339 would be even better.)

It'd be nice to have a `parse_rfc3339()` function that is stricter than `parse_datetime()` and raises if it gets input that's valid ISO 8601 but not valid RFC 3339.

issue_title: FileNotFoundError when cloning a repo
issue_body:
You're doing `git = Git(os.getcwd())` in https://github.com/gitpython-developers/GitPython/blob/master/git/repo/base.py#L979 although the cwd has no relevance to the cloning process as far as I can tell.

The expected behaviour would be to silently ignore the cwd not existing. (I.e. not call getcwd which throws the exception.)

We're unsure why the cwd doesn't exist (we're sure it can not exist though!) in our case but I think this'd better be fixed upstream instead of us doing a workaround.

Upstream issue: https://gitlab.com/gitmate/open-source/gitmate-2/issues/344

issue_title: HTTP 400 when following a 301 on /api/*
issue_body:
Discovered what I think is a bug.

This is trying to create a new site:

```json
$ curl http://localhost:8990/api/sites -H "X-NSoT-Email: codey@me.com" -X POST -d '{"name": "new issue", "description": ""}' -L -H "Content-Type: application/json" -s | jq .
{
  "status": "error",
  "error": {
    "message": {
      "name": [
        "This field is required."
      ]
    },
    "code": 400
  }
}

```

Considering that is part of the payload I provided, weird. Taking a closer look with `-i`:

```shell
$ curl http://localhost:8990/api/sites -H "X-NSoT-Email: codey@me.com" -X POST -d '{"name": "new issue", "description": ""}' -L -H "Content-Type: application/json" -i      
HTTP/1.1 301 MOVED PERMANENTLY
Server: gunicorn/19.3.0
Date: Sun, 27 Mar 2016 04:42:47 GMT
Connection: keep-alive
Transfer-Encoding: chunked
X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN
Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
Location: http://localhost:8990/api/sites/

HTTP/1.1 400 BAD REQUEST
Server: gunicorn/19.3.0
Date: Sun, 27 Mar 2016 04:42:47 GMT
Connection: keep-alive
Transfer-Encoding: chunked
Vary: Accept, Cookie
X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN
Content-Type: application/json
Allow: GET, POST, HEAD, OPTIONS
Set-Cookie: _xsrf=vKlDJJetzibyOttwlftVCYFTSkTrgT9U; expires=Sun, 26-Mar-2017 04:42:47 GMT; Max-Age=31449600; Path=/
Set-Cookie: sessionid=6fb55uq7lerle2q120yb9nsqjzho2wn5; expires=Sun, 10-Apr-2016 04:42:47 GMT; httponly; Max-Age=1209600; Path=/

{"status":"error","error":{"message":{"name":["This field is required."]},"code":400}}% 
```

Ah, I'm following a 301 because the actual views contain trailing slashes for this API. Still looks sane though... Trying directly to the exactly route path:

```json
$ curl http://localhost:8990/api/sites/ -H "X-NSoT-Email: codey@me.com" -X POST -d '{"name": "new issue", "description": ""}' -L -H "Content-Type: application/json" | jq .   
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed
100   117    0    77  100    40   1312    682 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--  1327
{
  "status": "ok",
  "data": {
    "site": {
      "id": 11,
      "name": "new issue",
      "description": ""
    }
  }
}
```

This is communicating directly against NSoT instance so no weird proxy in the middle. This is an issue itself, but mainly mentioning because creating a site fails using the `pynsot` API - likely because `slumber` doesn't add a trailing slash when resolving python -> URL.

```python
In [43]: c = get_api_client()

In [44]: c.sites.post({'name': 'ha'})
INFO:requests.packages.urllib3.connectionpool:Starting new HTTP connection (1): localhost
DEBUG:requests.packages.urllib3.connectionpool:"POST /api/sites/ HTTP/1.1" 400 None

```

issue_body:
In the Mission Pinball Project we check out two repositories:
src/mpf/ (https://github.com/missionpinball/mpf/)
src/mpf-mc/ (https://github.com/missionpinball/mpf-mc/)

I ran type annotations from within src/mpf/. Most annotations look fine in type_info.json (there is actually an mpf folder below the other src/mpf):

```
    {
        "func_name": "Show._add_token",
        "path": "mpf/assets/show.py",
        "type_comments": [
            "(str, List[Union[int, str]], str) -> None"
        ],
        "line": 249,
        "samples": 250
    },
```

However, some annotations look strange: 

```
    {   
        "func_name": "BcpProcessor",
        "path": "mc/mpfmc/core/bcp_processor.py",
        "type_comments": [
            "() -> None"
        ],
        "line": 15,
        "samples": 1
    },
```

There is no mc folder below src/mpf/. It refers to file in src/mpf-mc/ (there is a mpfmc folder in src/mpf-mc). However, I have no idea why this is "mc/mpfmc/" instead of "../mpf-mc/mpfmc/".

issue_title: Support holding shift and control when using `Click Element`
Get Matching Xpath Count returns string instead of int
issue_body:
This "feature" has been present AFAIK since about the first check-in. I tripped up over this when attempting to add a value to the return and got a type error.

Two suggested approaches:

1. Change the documentation, per [this pull request](https://github.com/robotframework/Selenium2Library/pull/714).
2. As an optional keyword argument, allow for an `int` return (default would remain `str`). I have not included a code example/pull for that.

issue_title: how to validate that the text exists on the page, not in the HTML code
issue_body: Yeah, we can check the text on page using the step **Wait Until Page Contains** But this won't work in the following case: HTML — Experience CSS — text-transform: uppercase How it looks on the page — EXPERIENCE My step: Wait Until Page Contains EXPERIENCE Result: FAIL Text 'EXPERIENCE' did not appear in 5 seconds Why? Because Experience!=EXPERIENCE We need to check the text as it appears on the page (not in the HTML code).

issue_title: Docs search very hard to find and broken
issue_body:
First of all it's pretty hard to find the search at all in the docs (would be useful in the sidebar) but there is a link at the bottom of the docs homepage: https://docs.pipenv.org/

Which points to this page: https://docs.pipenv.org/search/

Actually searching for something like this: https://docs.pipenv.org/search/?q=test
Results in links like these which only result in 404s: https://docs.pipenv.org/en/latest/advanced?highlight=test

The problem seems to be that the search index uses the standard readthedocs urls containing the versions (`/en/latest/`) instead of using the root. I'm not entirely sure if this bug is restricted to this project's configuration or if it's an upstream bug.

issue_title: export requirement packages with source tar.gz
issue_body: hello, in development environment network is available, but many production environment offline. I want export all requirements to directory `packages` include like `requests-2.19.1.tar.gz`, so i can `python setup.py install` in any case.

issue_title: pipenv incorrectly resolves dev-dependencies
issue_body:
When installing `dev` dependencies some packages receive wrong versions.
These packages seem to ignore constraints defined in `Pipfile`.

##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type: macos, 10.11.6
1. Python version: `$ python -V`, 3.6.2
1. Pipenv version: `$ pipenv --version`, 9.0.1

##### Expected result

I expect that `django==1.11` will be installed.

##### Actual result

`django==2.0` is installed.

##### Steps to replicate

Pipfile

```toml
[[source]]

url = "https://pypi.python.org/simple"
verify_ssl = true


[requires]

python_version = '3.6'


[dev-packages]

django-debug-toolbar = "*"


[packages]

django = "<1.12"
```

issue_title: UnicodeDecodeError with Python3 in Docker container
issue_body:
Trying to install packages via pipenv inside a Python 3.5/3.6 Docker container throws a UnicodeDecodeError.
Note: I'm installing from a specific commit, otherwise it's not finding the correct python binary as mentioned in #1002  - I know this isn't a release version, but figured it best to bring this up just in case that's about to be.

##### Describe your environment

1. macOS 10.13.2
2. Python versions: `$ 3.5x` or `$ 3.6x` 
3. Pipenv version: See the commit hash in the Dockerfile
4. Docker version: 17.12.0-ce-mac49 (21995) 

##### Expected result

Expected some packages to be installed.

##### Actual result

```
Failed building wheel for markupsafe
Exception:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/root/.local/share/virtualenvs/app-4PlAip0Q/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pip/compat/__init__.py", line 73, in console_to_str
    return s.decode(sys.__stdout__.encoding)

  File "/root/.local/share/virtualenvs/app-4PlAip0Q/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pip/compat/__init__.py", line 75, in console_to_str
    return s.decode('utf_8')
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0xb5 in position 1: invalid start byte
```

##### Steps to replicate

`docker build` this file

```
FROM python:3.6 as builder

RUN set -ex && pip install pip git+git://github.com/pypa/pipenv.git@8378a1b104f2d817790a05da370bef0a1b00f452 --upgrade

`ENV LC_ALL C.UTF-8` and `ENV LANG C.UTF-8`
Apologies if this is either a) a known issue or b) my own stupidity.

issue_title: Wrong lock file generated with impossible lock version.
issue_body:
I'm using pipenv to install `azure-cosmosdb-table`, it requires a lib named `futures` of any version, this lib's newest version `3.2.0` is only available for Python2. issue_title: Fails on installing package with conditional version
issue_body:
I am installing a package with a dependency that have a python versión condition.  It depends on a django version for python2 and another for python3 (the version I am using) but pipenv are choosing the py2 version of the package. I tried to force the new one installation but it fail.
`pipenv install drf-yasg 'django>2' `

If I install drf-yasg with pip in a fresh virtualenv it install django>=2.0.0  so it's not a pip problem, is a problem of pipenv when locking.

issue_title: Add ability to pass arguments to the safety library when running pipenv check
issue_body:
There's a particular CVE that is being raised for our project when running `pipenv check` that we'd like to ignore. The safety library provides the `--ignore` argument for this purpose, but the `check` command doesn't seem to support passing args down to the safety library.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

I'd like some method for passing arguments down to the safety library, though I don't know what UI would be best. It could involve copying args that safety supports onto check. Or an argument on check like `safety-args` that'll group a string under a single argument to be expanded and passed down.

Another solution could be environment variables `SAFETY_IGNORE` for example, but the underlying library does not support that as a variable, so it'd have to be collected and passed down anyway.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

Running safety directly and bypassing pipenv check, but that's not a nice alternative.

issue_title: Issue getting pipenv to run in a python3 script.
issue_body:
Hi I'm currently having issues getting python3 to recognise my Pipenv virtualenv.

I have created a simple scripts for executing tests within `pipenv` along the lines of `pipenv run pytest ...`. The scripts is packaged into a click application using a `setup.py` file and installed by running `pip3 install -e .` in the click applications directory. However when I try to run this application `script test` it fails saying:
```
Creating a virtualenv for this project…
Using /usr/local/Cellar/python/3.6.4_4/bin/python3.6 (3.6.4) to create virtualenv…
⠋/usr/local/opt/python/bin/python3.6: Error while finding module specification for 'pipenv.pew' (ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pipenv')
```
which it shouldn't as a pipenv environment already exists. I have noticed that when running the following:
`python -c "from subprocess import call; call(['pipenv', '--venv'])"`
`python3 -c "from subprocess import call; call(['pipenv', '--venv'])"`
python shows the virtualenv, but python3 fails saying `No virtualenv has been created for this project yet!`
Any help would be greatfully appreciated!

The click applciation is along the lines of:
```
import click
from subprocess import call

@click.group()
def cli:
  pass

@click.command()
def test():
  call(['pipenv', 'run', 'pytest', ...])
```
The script is called using: `script test`

issue_body:
How about the feature for skipping some packages selectively, I mean something like this:

```
pipenv install --skip "mypy"
```

Why?

Some packages not incompatible with some implementations of Python (`mypy` does not work with `PyPy` for example) and it would be awesome to have feature for skipping such packages.

For example on building on CI:

```
  - if [[ $TRAVIS_PYTHON_VERSION == "pypy3" ]]; then pipenv install --dev --skip "mypy"; fi
```

issue_title: Possible usecase for multiple groups.
issue_body:
Hi. I remember that we agree to do only default and dev group at this moment.

I want to explain my seeing of this feature if you decide to revisit this issue in the future.

We have customer who runs our web application written in python in its own cloud. Our application depends on few closed source packages developed for our customer by another team. We don't have access to this packages, but know provided api.

For testing purpose this team create stubs packages which we can install from PyPI installation running by our customer.

Before deploying new app release to staging infrastructure new code is reviewed by customer programmers team. In staging environment customer devops install real packages with known names from another PyPI instance running at customer staging environment. After QA engineers approved new release same installation shipped to the production environment where customer PyPI instance running on another url.

So it's good to have something that can handle this tricky setup easily. I see it like

```
[packages]
customer_package_foo = "*"
customer_package_bar = "*"

[packages:staging]
<<inherit packages from previous section>>
<<pin real dependencies of foo and bar packages>>
<<provide staging pypi url>>

[package:prod]
<<inherit packages from staging>>
<<provide prod pypi url>>
```

issue_title: RFC: Selective system-site-packages
issue_body:
With certain packages such as PyQt or the SciPy stack, it's impractical to use them inside a virtualenv for various reasons:

- They might be hard to build, or take a long time to do so
- They use non-standard setup methods (PyQt) which makes it even harder to set them up in a virtualenv.

The obvious solution for this is using `--system-site-packages`, but that provides poor isolation from the system Python install, which I'd like.

The solution I used for PyQt is to write a script, [link_pyqt.py](https://github.com/The-Compiler/qutebrowser/blob/master/scripts/link_pyqt.py) to symlink PyQt into the virtualenv.

I think this could be simplified and generalized a bit, and then maybe included into virtualenv so you could do something like `--system-site-packages=PyQt5,NumPy` and it'd link/copy (depending on the os and 
`--always-copy`) those packages from the system Python into the virtualenv.

What do you think?

/cc @nicoddemus @raoulb

I have a very strange problem, and it appears only on some of the machines I'm using.
So I have a class that handles producer. In I create a producer attribute like this:
```python
self.producer = self.topic.get_sync_producer(max_request_size=max_request_size)
```
Sending data is done by a simple method:
```python
def save(self, data):
    self.producer.produce(data)
```

Now, the problem is that when there is no data, program freezes with logging message:
`2016-12-06 13:12:22 [DEBUG] "Blocking until all messages are sent"`

```python
def __del__(self):
    if self.producer._owned_brokers is not None:
            for owned_broker in self.producer._owned_brokers is not None:
                owned_broker.stop()
```

issue_title: Evaluate pex as a deployment option for streamparse

I don't think it would be too hard to get it working even with a current version of streamparse, based on the rough description in the docs.

issue_title: Package name should NOT be "analytics", rename to segment/segmentio
issue_body:
The top-level package name really should be something unique, like **segmentio** or something. If you have another package or module loaded in your Python load path named analytics, a very common word, there is a name-space collision and it's possible (or very difficult) to resolve in Python

```python
# import analytics
import segmentio
```

issue_title: Spatial filter seems to match positions outside region
issue_body:
The spatial filter seems to match positions which are not part of the specified region.  In the following (very slow) test script, I test points over the whole sky using a 1 degree grid.  The output has, as well as the two expected circles, lines at RA = +/- 90 degrees and a few other scattered points.  This was with Astropy 1.0.6 and the current master version of pyregion from this repository.

``` python
from __future__ import print_function                                           
from astropy.units import degree                                                
from astropy.wcs import WCS                                                     
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt                                                 
import pyregion                                                                 

region = pyregion.parse('''                                                     
    fk5                                                                         
    circle(0:00:00.000,00:00:00.00,36000")                                      
    circle(3:00:00.000,75:00:00.00,36000")                                      
''')                                                                            

ras = []                                                                        
decs = []                                                                       

for ra in range(-179, 180):                                                     
    for dec in range (-89, 90):                                                 
        wcs = WCS(naxis=2)                                                      
        wcs.wcs.radesys = 'ICRS'                                                
        wcs.wcs.ctype = ['RA---TAN', 'DEC--TAN']                                
        wcs.wcs.cunit = [degree, degree]                                        
        wcs.wcs.crpix = [1, 1]                                                  
        wcs.wcs.cdelt = [-0.0003, 0.0003]                                       
        wcs.wcs.crval = [ra, dec]                                               

        if region.get_filter(header=wcs.to_header()).inside1(1, 1):             
            #print(ra, dec)                                                     
            ras.append(ra)                                                      
            decs.append(dec)                                                    

plt.scatter(ras, decs)                                                          
plt.xlim(-180, 180)                                                             
plt.ylim(-90, 90)                                                               
plt.show() 
```

![figure_1](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1649796/11386855/8b918740-92c9-11e5-9300-73ecce29c0b4.png)

issue_title: Performance issue with centroid
issue_body:
I have been trying to use  `photutils.detection.find_peaks` function with the `subpixel=True` option and ran into a performance issue which prevent me to use it in a Monte Carlo simulation. I tracked this down to the `photutils.centroid.fit_2dgaussian` which use `astropy.modeling` to fit a custom 2d gaussian + constant model at each of the peaks.

Looking at `photutils.centroid.GaussianConst2D` class I wondered if this custom made compound was the culprit, so I ran a little benchmark to compare it to other definition of compound models and utilmately to `scipy.optimize.curve_fit`.  [Gist here](https://gist.github.com/abeelen/f3bf445cb0628dc4fe7a4fc3a89be28c) 

It turns out that for small data set, which `find_peaks` is doing, the overhead is huge :
* 13x compared to `curve_fit`,
* or even 3x compared to a simple  `astropy.modeling.custom_model` decorator

Things are worse when adding a little bit of noise.

I could work out a PR to write a proper model for `GaussianConst2D` but i would still be (much, 6x) slower than a simple curve_fit. What would be the best way to proceed here ?

issue_title: python setup.py test does not install packages in tests_require
issue_body:
I found this issue in CCDproc, but I report it here because it seems to be an issue of astropy-helpers.

ccdproc requires the module pytest-capturelog and specifies that in its `tests_require` in setup.py.
( https://github.com/astropy/ccdproc/blob/master/setup.py#L98 )
I don't have pytest-capturelog installed on my machine and the corresponding tests fail with

```
        fixture 'caplog' not found                                               
        available fixtures: tmpdir_factory, pytestconfig, ccd_data, cache, recwarn, monkeypatch, triage_setup, record_xml_property, capfd, capsys, tmpdir         
        use 'py.test --fixtures [testpath]' for help on them.                    
```

Theses failures go away after manually installing pytest-capturelog.
Thus, it seems that either `python setup.py test` does not install the required dependencies or it installs them in a location where they are not found.
(My understanding is that `tests_require` dependencies are installed in a temporary directory only, but I might be wrong.)

There are two obvious workarounds: Installs test dependencies by hand or put them into `install_requires` even though they are only needed for testing.
(Note that this does not show up as an issue in the travis, because this dependency is installed by hand here https://github.com/astropy/ccdproc/blob/master/.travis.yml#L101 )

cc: @mwcraig

numpy deprecation: Should `regular_grid` return a tuple?
issue_body:
https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/f21c52bc248b59197129d60b97285e1579fee6bf/skimage/util/_regular_grid.py#L21-L25

Numpy decided to deprecacte multi-indexing with anything other than a [tuple](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/release/1.15.0-notes.rst#deprecations).

regular grid currently returns a list. It might be useful to keep up with numpy's API, and as such, we should return a tuple.

For example, just below in `_regular_grid.py`
https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/f21c52bc248b59197129d60b97285e1579fee6bf/skimage/util/_regular_grid.py#L101-L104

uses the output directly to index a numpy array. 

Unfortunately, lists are not tuples, and this would cause a breaking change in the ~~(internal)~~ API.

How should we proceed with this?

Ideas I have:
  1. Check the numpy version, if greater than `1.15` then we can return a tuple.
  2. Return a list until numpy decides to change their implementation
  3. Return a tuple instead of a list.

I kinda like 3, but doesn't seem prudent.

Edit: it seems that this is actually an external facing API.

issue_title: The "custom region demo" doesn't do what it says it does
issue_body:
There are a couple of issues in:

/examples/network/custom_region/identity_region.py
/examples/network/custom_region_demo.py

## Issue 1

It says it passes "the encoded representation to an Identity Region". But it actually passes the unencoded input. 

This line uses the wrong source on the RecordSensor:

~~~python
  network.link("sensor", "identityRegion", "UniformLink", "",
               srcOutput="sourceOut", destInput="in")
~~~

It should use srcOutput "dataOut", or just use the default.

~~~python
  network.link("sensor", "identityRegion", "UniformLink", "")
~~~

## Issue 2

The IdentityRegion is not actually writing to the output. It's reassigning the output variable to reference a totally different array.

The fix: Change

~~~python
outputs["out"] = inputs["in"]
~~~

to

~~~python
outputs["out"][:] = inputs["in"]
~~~


## Issue 3

Now that we've solved those, we need to put the correct `dataWidth` in the IdentityRegion.

Basically, change the line:

~~~python
network.addRegion("identityRegion", "py.IdentityRegion", json.dumps(I_PARAMS))
~~~

to:

~~~python
network.addRegion("identityRegion", "py.IdentityRegion", json.dumps({
        "dataWidth": sensor.encoder.getWidth(),
    }))
~~~

issue_title: Bug when package name has a dash
issue_body: Pyramid's .ini scaffolds have ```use = egg:{{project}}``` in them, but cornice/scaffolds/cornice/+package+.ini_tmpl has ```use = egg:{{package}}```. This creates a .ini file with an incorrect egg reference, which causes a ```pkg_resources.DistributionNotFound``` exception when pserve is run. I've tested that replacing {{package}} with {{project}} does fix this issue, and I can only assume that nothing else will break with this change.

issue_title: Documenting Resources with Sphinx integration
issue_body:
I have a Pyramid+Cornice application and I'm using `resources` rather than `services`. I wonder if it is possible to generate Sphinx documentation for resources in a same way like with services?

I managed to generate some sort of documentation but it seems like majority of features are not available for resources, for example, documenting Pyramid code in this way:

    @resource(collection_path='/parse/', path='/parse/{id}', cors_origins=('*',), description="Temporary description.")
    class Parser(object):
    
        @view(renderer='json')
        def collection_post(self):
            """
            Some description for this function...
            :param str smth: Some parameter
            :return: Returns something
            """
            return

and building Sphinx documentation like this:

    .. cornice-autodoc::
       :modules: my_app.views.views_parser
       :ignore: parser

will result in few unnecessary and few missing parts in documentation:

 - service names (actually resource names) are messed up,
 - function `collection_post` itself is not documented,
 - no need to document both `collection_path` and `path` from `resource` declaration):

________________
<sub><sup>(example snippet from generated documentation)</sup></sub>
# Collection_Parser service at /parse/
Temporary description.

## POST
**Response: json**

# Parser service at /parse/{id}
Temporary description.

________________
What I would like to know is:  

 - how to change names generated in documentation (`Parser` instead of `Collection_Parser`),
 - how to 'ignore' some modules with `:ignore:` statement (e.g. I don't want to document `/parse/{id}` part),
 - how to document functions within Resources, since putting a comment block is just being ignored (`collection_post` in my case)

...and many other things...  
It seems like this is either impossible or not documented at all.  
Thanks for any help, tip or advice!

issue_title: tests: replace `sleep` with flush + refresh
issue_body:
Many tests throughout Invenio tend to call `sleep(2)` after any Elasticsearch-related actions (indexing, deleting, creating indices, etc). This significantly affects total testing time and is non-deterministic, since in case of some Travis build/container slows down because of a memory swap or disk I/O throttling, these 2 seconds might not be enough, causing the build to fail.

Instead of that calling `current_search.flush_and_refresh(index_name)` is a valid alternative that programmatically guarantees that all Elasticsearch actions have been committed and written to disk.

  File "<console>", line 1, in <module>
AssertionError: 2016/04/21 13:05:00 GMT+0 is not equal to 2016/04/21 13:05:00 GMT+2

In summary, one digit number for hour, minute or second always returns TZ GMT+0. Two digit number format of time (hh:mm:ss) returns local TZ. Is this a bug or feature?

issue_title: Add test run with z3c.pt dependency
issue_body: There is test infrastructure to run the tests against `z3c.pt` aka `Chameleon` but it is only used if the extra named `extra` is installed. (`buildout.cfg` has the (now disabled) `test-with-z3cpt` section)

issue_title: Fall back to msgfmt(1) when python-gettext is unavailable
issue_body:
According to PyPI, python-gettext was last uploaded in February 2013.  It's unclear whether the package is still supported.  It doesn't --and isn't likely to-- exist in the Debian archive.  But that's fine because its functionality can be reproduced well enough to pass the test suite with a replacement made of subprocess and msgfmt(1).  We'll be using something like the following patch in Debian (it's not even worth writing a pull request ;)

```
--- a/src/zope/i18n/compile.py
+++ b/src/zope/i18n/compile.py
@@ -15,14 +15,17 @@
 
 def compile_mo_file(domain, lc_messages_path):
     """Creates or updates a mo file in the locales folder."""
-    if not HAS_PYTHON_GETTEXT:
-        logger.critical("Unable to compile messages: Python `gettext` library missing.")
-        return
-
     base = join(lc_messages_path, domain)
     pofile = str(base + '.po')
     mofile = str(base + '.mo')
 
+    if not HAS_PYTHON_GETTEXT:
+        #logger.critical("Unable to compile messages: Python `gettext` library missing.")
+        # 2015-06-22 barry@debian.org: Fall back to msgfmt(1).
+        from subprocess import check_call
+        check_call(['msgfmt', '-o', mofile, pofile])
+        return
+
     po_mtime = 0
     try:
         po_mtime = os.stat(pofile)[ST_MTIME]
```

issue_title: Minor typo in docstring for `query.IndexQuery.__init__()`

issue_title: GenericSetup upgradeStep ZCML discriminator should include the profile
issue_body:
In https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope-cmf/+bug/924721, @rpatterson reported:

> It appears the ZCML handler for the upgradeSetp directive doesn't include the profile id in the discriminator. This means that if you register the same handler for two different profiles but with the same source and destination versions, you get a ConfigurationConflictError.

issue_title: Android app freeze on screen rotation (again?)
issue_body:
Seems that with python 3 crystax ndk version 10.3.2, and latest kivy master, and the latest python for android master, screen rotation will freeze an app... no crash, no error, just freeze.
This also manifests when a 'landscape' orientation app is resumed from screen off, can see the screen rotating briefly and it freezes.
As with: https://github.com/kivy/python-for-android/issues/379
setting android.minapi=13 will fix it.

Attached are a simple buildozer.spec and main.py if anyone wants to test it out, tho you'll have to download the crystax ndk and edit the spec to point to it.
[kivytestapp.zip](https://github.com/kivy/python-for-android/files/1498638/kivytestapp.zip)

issue_title: NE3: Striding of complex_functions.hpp
issue_body:
This one is easy, we need the complex functions to have a separate branch for strided operations.  This can be done in the same was as `real_functions.cpp`.  Note that `.real` and `.imag` return strided `float` views of the underlying `complex` array.

One concern unveiled in testing is that complex division with `float32` yields different values between NumExpr3 and NumPy.  The implementation of NumExpr3’s complex division is naïve. Most higher precision complex division algorithms, such as Smith’s algorithm, are branched based on the complex phase of the involved numbers.  Ideally we would prefer a branchless algorithm, if one can be sourced, as it is likely to be much faster within the confines of vectorized operations.

issue_title: ENH: Support retrieval of SEC EDGAR indices.
issue_body:
The SEC has a large database of company filings (e.g., annual reports, proxy statements), called [EDGAR](http://www.sec.gov/edgar/searchedgar/companysearch.html), that it makes publicly accessible. They also provide a set of (huge) [indices](http://www.sec.gov/edgar/indices/fullindex.htm) to all of those filings, and it would be really helpful to be able to pull that index, filter it down to a set of companies, date ranges, and filing types, and then use the filename fields to pull the data.

As a first step, I'd like to propose support for pulling an index file from the SEC, parsing it, and returning it as a DataFrame. I plan on working on it myself, so I'm mostly interested in whether it's a good fit. I think it so, but I'd like to gauge interest and solicit some feedback.

The data is different than a lot of stuff in `pandas_datareader` in that it's a step toward pulling text in addition to numeric data. That said, a lot of the research or data projects (mine for sure) that use the financial and industry data available with `pandas_datareader` may also be interested in this kind of data (or descriptive stats of the filings in the indices).

Later on, I think it would be nice to support pulling documents themselves, but I'd like to start with the indices for now.

issue_title: qtpy.__version__ should be QtPy version, not Qt version
issue_body:
I think that ``__version__`` should be set to the actual QtPy version, otherwise packages won't be able to easily check for example that QtPy is recent enough to include certain patches. I think we could then make the Qt and other versions available via e.g.

```
qtpy.QT_VERSION
qtpy.PYSIDE_VERSION
qtpy.PYQT4_VERSION
qtpy.PYQT5_VERSION
```

I'd be happy to include a quick PR to include in QtPy 1.1 if others agree?

issue_title: vector.data() not supported on MS VC++ 9.0
issue_body:
`std::vector::data` is defined in C+11, so Microsoft Visual C++ 9.0 (aka Visual Studio 2008) does not support it and raises the following error:

```
python/brotlimodule.cc(194) : error C2039: 'data' : is not a member of 'std::vector<_Ty>'
        with
        [
            _Ty=uint8_t
        ]
```

The (uglier) method `&vector[0]` could be used instead of `vector.data()` to get the pointer to the underlying array (provided it isn't empty).

As you know, Python 2.7 for Windows is still compiled with Visual Studio 2008, so all extension modules should in theory be compiled using the same MSVC compiler version. After VS2008 was discontinued, Microsoft released an ad-hoc "Visual C++ Compiler for Python 2.7" (http://aka.ms/vcpython27) meant to be used for compiling extension for Windows Python 2.7.

In the current Brotli's `setup.py`, I added a patch to force distutils use Visual Studio 2010 for Python 2.7. This seems to work well so far, despite many people warn against mixing different C runtimes versions between the interpreter and the extension modules.

Since you recently added support for C+98, it'd be nice to also try support the old Microsoft compiler.

Thank you.

issue_title: Rename prefix
issue_body:
Love the Python library to make RDF! Keep it going.

One issue I don't know how to fix and I didn't find explicitly in the RDFLib 5.0.0 documentation is renaming a prefix. For example, I have been given a graph from an external source and don't like the way they did their prefixes. Therefore I want to rename the prefix at the start and in the graph. 
This is what I parsed from a link:

``` RDF file in n3 format (INCORRECT)
@prefix rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>.
@prefix rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> .
@prefix n80: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>.
n80:Geometry rdf:type <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#Class>;
    rdfs:isDefinedBy <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
.
n80:hasGeometry rdf:type <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#ObjectProperty>;
    rdfs:isDefinedBy <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
.
```
This is where I want to go:
``` RDF file in n3 format (CORRECT!)
@prefix rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>.
@prefix rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> .
@prefix geo: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>.
geo:Geometry rdf:type <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#Class>;
    rdfs:isDefinedBy <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
.
geo:hasGeometry rdf:type <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#ObjectProperty>;
    rdfs:isDefinedBy <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
.
```
This is how I tried to solve it, but which didn't work.

``` Python Script
from rdflib import Namespace, Graph

buildings_ontology = 'https://bag.basisregistraties.overheid.nl/query/model?format=ttl'
g = Graph()
g.parse(buildings_ontology, format = 'n3')
geo = Namespace('http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#')
g.namespace_manager.bind('n80', geo, override = True, replace=True)
```
How would you rename the prefix at the start and in the graph?

Since I think there is no method or function that does that, I made an issue on GitHub.
For now this issue is fixed by downloading as text and replacing 'n80:' for 'geo:'.

Paul

issue_title: Change default engine to the new dependency-free one introduced in #77
issue_body:
The exporter now has an engine that doesn't have any dependencies. It should probably be the one activated by default, to make the whole package dependency-free unless another engine is configured. It also happens to be faster, and less memory-hungry.

This is a breaking change though, no matter how little difference there is with the output of the current default (html5lib + BeautifulSoup), so should be part of the 2.0.0 release.

As part of this change, it will also be necessary to move the html5lib / BS4 dependencies to a separate extra like for lxml (`pip install draftjs_exporter[html5lib]`), as well as update the documentation.

issue_title: How difficult would it be to expand the client to support changing a user's password?
issue_body:
I noticed changing a user's password is not currently supported. Doing so returns the error "Password modification is not yet supported", per this code:

https://github.com/irods/python-irodsclient/blob/master/irods/manager/user_manager.py#L61-L62

Is there something complex about that command, that requires intentionally looking for it and returning an error, while the other commands fall through to the `GeneralAdminRequest`?

I'm asking because I'd like to use the `python-irodsclient` in a Django server, but changing a user's password is one of the critical commands I need, for which the lack of support is currently preventing me from using the client. The interim approach I'm following involves using Python's `subprocess` module to invoke `iCommands` directly or invoke scripts that invoke `iCommands`. Admittedly not a great solution, but working for the moment.

I'd be willing to submit a PR to add support for changing a user's password, if you can provide guidance on why it's not currently implemented, and how difficult it might be for me to include it, or maybe some pointers on what I should be aware of in attempting to do so.

Is that something you'd be willing to advise on?

issue_title: OPTIONS generated by CORS doen't work correctly in local
issue_body:
First of all, thank you for this awesome framework. Really easy to use, I like it a lot.

I'm developing an API that has to accept CORS and i'm having troubles with the OPTIONS methods.

The problem is easy to reproduce:
In app.py
```
...
@app.route('/test', methods=['POST'], cors=True)
def test_cors():
  return {'hello': 'world'}

```
Then, run it in local: `chalice local --port=8100`

When you try to do the first OPTIONS curl, everything works =)

```
curl -H "Origin: http://example.com" \                                                      
  -H "Access-Control-Request-Method: POST" \
  -H "Access-Control-Request-Headers: X-Requested-With" \
  -X OPTIONS --verbose \
  http://localhost:8100/test
```
Response:
```
*   Trying ::1...
* TCP_NODELAY set
* Connection failed
* connect to ::1 port 8100 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1...
* TCP_NODELAY set
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8100 (#0)
> OPTIONS /test HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8100
> User-Agent: curl/7.51.0
> Accept: */*
> Origin: http://example.com
> Access-Control-Request-Method: POST
> Access-Control-Request-Headers: X-Requested-With
>
* HTTP 1.0, assume close after body
< HTTP/1.0 200 OK
< Server: BaseHTTP/0.6 Python/3.6.0
< Date: Thu, 07 Sep 2017 16:02:02 GMT
< Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
< Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization,Content-Type,X-Amz-Date,X-Amz-Security-Token,X-Api-Key
< Access-Control-Allow-Methods: POST,OPTIONS
<
* Curl_http_done: called premature == 0
* Closing connection 0
```

But the next OPTIONS curl will not end never =(

```
*   Trying ::1...
* TCP_NODELAY set
* Connection failed
* connect to ::1 port 8100 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1...
* TCP_NODELAY set
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8100 (#0)
> OPTIONS /test HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8100
> User-Agent: curl/7.51.0
> Accept: */*
> Origin: http://example.com
> Access-Control-Request-Method: POST
> Access-Control-Request-Headers: X-Requested-With
>
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Server: BaseHTTP/0.6 Python/3.6.0
< Date: Thu, 07 Sep 2017 16:02:03 GMT
< Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
< Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization,Content-Type,X-Amz-Date,X-Amz-Security-Token,X-Api-Key
< Access-Control-Allow-Methods: POST,OPTIONS
* no chunk, no close, no size. Assume close to signal end
```

It stops in  `* no chunk, no close, no size. Assume close to signal end`. It seems like the server only send the close signal only in the first call. If you stop the second curl and try it again, it wont stop neither.
 
Do you have the same problem? Can you reproduce it?

P.S.: I'm using macOS, but I hope that is not a problem to do the local development =P

issue_title: Chalice Package File Permissions
issue_body:
Hey guys, when I run `chalice deploy` on my laptop everything works great but when I run it on a Heroku instance it creates this error when calling the function.

```
START RequestId: 520c9d03-617b-11e8-bedb-6bcc2dc21d2d Version: $LATEST
module initialization error: [Errno 13] Permission denied: '/var/task/app.py'

END RequestId: 520c9d03-617b-11e8-bedb-6bcc2dc21d2d
REPORT RequestId: 520c9d03-617b-11e8-bedb-6bcc2dc21d2d    Duration: 293.22 ms    Billed Duration: 300 ms     Memory Size: 128 MB    Max Memory Used: 21 MB    
module initialization error
[Errno 13] Permission denied: '/var/task/app.py'
```

I am guessing this is a permissions error. So I tried `chmod -R 777` on the repo and it partially fixed the problem by granting permissions to existing files in the repos. Now the problem is that all the other files like the `chalice` folder and `vendor` folder all do not have proper permissions because the heroku instance default permissions do now allow `rwx`. 

```
START RequestId: 520c9d03-617b-11e8-bedb-6bcc2dc21d2d Version: $LATEST
module initialization error: [Errno 13] Permission denied: '/var/task/chalice/__init__.py'

module initialization error
[Errno 13] Permission denied: '/var/task/chalice/__init__.py'
```

Would it be possible to chmod everything that goes into the package zip the chalice library creates. This way all the files have the same permissions and should run on AWS lambda?

issue_title: Precompiled wheels for macOS with linked dynamic library
issue_body: Wheels can be built for macOS (#841), and the `delocate-wheel` tool is supposed to take `libdynet.dylib` and relink it into the wheel so that the `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH` workaround (#146) doesn't have to be used. However, when using `delocate-wheel`, even though `libdynet.dylib` is found, it is not relinked into the wheel. The cause for this problem might be that the DyNet Python files are not in a package, but just at the top level of the installation (matthew-brett/delocate#15).

issue_title: Test for a low ratio of direct metabolites in the biomass reaction
issue_body:
#### Problem description

> Some biomass precursors are taken from the media and directly consumed by the biomass reaction. It might not be a problem for some ions or metabolites with known auxotrophy, but when there are too many of these metabolites, it points to artefacts of gap-filling. This mostly happens in case of automatically-generated models. Starting with a complicated automatically generated biomass formulation followed by gap-filling (which basically tries to minimise the added reactions), would lead to many biomass metabolites being "direct mets". It might be useful to report the ratio of biomass metabolites that are direct mets.

- Parizad Babayi, comment on the manuscript.

issue_title: Monitoring: Write Timeseries Values Usage Plan
issue_body:
Currently the Monitoring client does not support writing timeseries values, though that's probably the canonical use case of the API.

@rimey is planning on adding it, I was hoping to add some samples sooner so he said I could start to spec it out, and whoever gets to it first will do the PR, but either way we should come to some consensus on the usage patterns first.

I know he was also concerned about #1732 since datetimes are pretty important in monitoring, but I'm not sure yet what's actionable. We should definitely minimize needs for user to play with timestamps.

Anyway high level overview of Monitoring API from my understanding

MetricDescriptor - This object describes a type of Metric, can be parametrized with LabelDescriptors
MonitoredResourceDescriptor - This object describes a type of thing to be monitored, can be parametrized with LabelDescriptors

Metric - A MetricDescriptor parametrized with labels
Resource - A ResourceDescriptor paramettrized with labels

TimeSeries - Metric + Resource + points. The points have intervals, although for GAUGE metrics (one of the MetricKind) that interval will have the same start and end time and usually be the current time. CUMULATIVE metrics will have a separate start and end time, with the end time usually being the current time. DELTA metrics can not be used for custom metrics, so I think can be ignored for the purpose of writing timeseries, because I think you only ever write custom metrics (non-custom metrics are the default ones written for you).

See the [current usage docs](http://googlecloudplatform.github.io/gcloud-python/stable/monitoring-usage.html) for more context.

I think the most common use case will be to write a custom GAUGE metric for the current time. 

` 
from gcloud import monitoring
client = monitoring.Client() 

# Create a Metric by its MetricDescriptor type name, assign labels as a dict
# Possibly doesn't exist but we won't check yet, unless they reload()
gauge_metric = client.metric('custom.googleapis.com/my_metric', labels={'response_code': 404})

# Create a Resource by its resource name, assign labels as dict
resource = client.resource('gce_instance', labels={'instance_id': 'my-instance', 'zone': 'us-central1-f'})

# Create and save the timeseries resource
client.write_timeseries(metric=metric, resource=resource, points=[1,2,3]) # API call

# GAUGE metrics should only specify end_time if now is not correct, start_time will default to end_time
client.write_timeseries(metric=metric, resource=resource, [4,5], end_time=get_a_time())

cumulative_metric = client.metric('custom.googleapis.com/my_cumulative_metric')

# end time always defaults to now
cilent.write_timeseries([7, 8, 9], start_time=get_start_time()) # API call
`

I was originally thinking something like 

`
# Abandoned pattern
# Add 3 time series points, all default to interval of start/end at now
timeseries = monitoring.TimeSeries(metric=metric, resource=resource)
timeseries.add_points([1, 2, 3])
# Write the 3 time series values
timeseries.save() # API call
`

The disadvantage is that when you want to add more points to the same Metric+Resource, you either have to create a new TimeSeries object anyway, or have strange semantics where you add points to an object you already created, even though really a new object is being created. So I think it's better if TimeSeries are immutable client-side and you just create them directly with the call on the client.

Another small point is the start_time and end_time defaults. The idea is that end_time always defaults to now, unless specified, and start_time always defaults to end_time, unless specified. This makes sense, though it might look slightly strange that sometimes we are only explicitly setting start_time (to indicate when a CUMULATIV metric starts) and other times only explicitly setting end_time (to change the entire interval for a GAUGE metric). I think it's ok though.

One of the big questions is how much client-side validation do we want to do. For example given a GAUGE metric descriptor, we know that startTime needs to equal endTime. However if we want to enforce this client-side, we have to pull down the metric descriptor first, introducing an extra API call. So it's probably best to just leave most of the validation server side.

Do we need an actual Label class or are dicts good enough? I think dicts are good enough.

cc / @sharbison3

issue_title: Should we map the '/projects/get' API
issue_body:

The resource consists of read-only information (quotas, mostly) about the project within DNS.

One strategy might be to add a `quotas` method to the client which fetched and returned the `quotas` sub-mapping for the project.

issue_title: Querying with order on empty dataset throws a 419
issue_body:
When querying an empty dataset that is empty with a sort order, for example:

```python
query = datastore.Query(kind='Book', order='title')
it = query.fetch(limit=limit, start_cursor=cursor)
entities, more_results, cursor = it.next_page()
```

It raises an exception:
```
PreconditionFailed: 412 no matching index found.
```

Once you create an entity in the dataset, the error disappears. It reappears when you delete all entities.

gcloud-node doesn't suffer from the same problem, as this just returns an empty array for an empty dataset:
```javascript
var q = ds.createQuery([kind])
      .limit(limit)
      .order('title')
      .start(token);
```

issue_title: spanner failed transaction being reused in next call to database.run_in_transaction()
issue_body:
Using google-cloud-spanner 0.28.0, when running the following:

```python
"""
DDL:

CREATE TABLE test (
	test_id STRING(MAX),
) PRIMARY KEY (test_id)
"""

instance = client.instance(INSTANCE_ID)
pool = spanner.BurstyPool(target_size=10)
database = instance.database(DATABASE_ID, pool=pool)

def unit_of_work(transaction, id):
    transaction.insert(
        table='test',
        columns=['test_id'],
        values=[
            [id],
        ])

print('#### inserting id_1')
database.run_in_transaction(unit_of_work, 'id_1')
print()

print('#### inserting id_1 again, expected to fail')
try:
    database.run_in_transaction(unit_of_work, 'id_1')

print()

issue_title: Speed up "push" builds.
issue_body:
Travis has two build types that we use: "push" and "pull request". On a "pull request" commit the `TRAVIS_BRANCH` env. var. tells us what we are diff-ing against and makes it easy to limit the build jobs based on the changed files. In "push" builds, the best we have is `TRAVIS_COMMIT_RANGE` but this is not a very dependable value. I propose we rely instead on the GitHub API to determine the changed files. 1. Try our best to parse the PR number from the commit message (if this fails, just bail and run every single test) 1. Verify the PR number by checking if the `TRAVIS_COMMIT` (and the `HEAD` commit in our local checkout) matches the `merge_commit_sha` in the PR endpoint: https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/pulls/2450 1. Once we have the PR, hit the PR files endpoint to get a list of changed files, e.g.: https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/pulls/2461/files ---- As an alternative to the first step, there is an "unpublished" API which helps associate a commit SHA with a PR. For example, see the parenthetical 2461 below: ![screen_shot_001](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/520669/19045350/3911fbb4-894d-11e6-8529-55f8d2fc272b.png) which tells us that 4e8afa8c3877376d7e9a6df79e6a272ae1cdfee7 came as part of #2461. The actual API returns an SVG with the payload containing ```
  • (#2461)
    • ``` The API simply requires the commit hash in `TRAVIS_COMMIT` (i.e. just the `HEAD` commit on our local checkout), e.g. https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/branch_commits/4e8afa8c3877376d7e9a6df79e6a272ae1cdfee7 ---- @tseaver @jonparrott PTAL and LMKWYT FYI this will build on top of work done for #2277.

issue_title: Pub / Sub Subscriber: CPU Usage eventually spikes to 100%
issue_labels: [{'id': 139685153, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20pubsub', 'name': 'api: pubsub', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604244, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p1', 'name': 'priority: p1', 'color': 'ffa03e', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438580, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': 'd93f0b', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
This is a distillation of this report from **many** other issues. Large thanks to @anorth2 and @dmontag for reporting and helping refine the issue.

Issues that have been partially resolved (except for the CPU spike) are being collapsed into this one:

- #4274 
- #4563 
- (more to come?)

----

**Core issue**: there is a [spinlock bug][1] in gRPC (present in all recent versions `1.6.x`, `1.7.x`, `1.8.1`). A "bandaid" [fix][2] exists but has not been merged (as of noon Pacific on December 15, 2017).

**Potential workaround**: compile `grpcio` from source ([e.g.][3]) while including the bandaid [fix][2] or just use the 64-bit `manylinux` [wheel][4] that I already created (I may be open to creating Mac OS X wheels, not sure about Windows)

[1]: https://github.com/grpc/grpc/issues/9688
[2]: https://github.com/grpc/grpc/pull/13665
[3]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/issues/4274#issuecomment-351863030
[4]: https://github.com/dhermes/google-cloud-pubsub-performance/blob/master/grpcio-1.7.4.dev1-cp36-cp36m-manylinux1_x86_64.whl

issue_title: Parameters and SecurityGroup
issue_body:
When running the following adaptation of ELB_Sample.py

```PYTHON
# Converted from ELBSample.template located at:
# http://aws.amazon.com/cloudformation/aws-cloudformation-templates/

from troposphere import Base64, FindInMap, GetAtt, GetAZs, Join, Output
from troposphere import Parameter, Ref, Template
import troposphere.ec2 as ec2
import troposphere.elasticloadbalancing as elb


def AddAMI(template):
    template.add_mapping("RegionMap", {
        "us-west-1": {"AMI": "ami-1af8ab72"},
        "us-east-1": {"AMI": "ami-1af8ab72"}

    })


def main():
    template = Template()
    AddAMI(template)

    # Add the Parameters
    keyname_param = template.add_parameter(Parameter(
        "KeyName",
        Type="String",
        Default="eod",
        Description="Name of an existing EC2 KeyPair to "
                    "enable SSH access to the instance",
    ))

    template.add_parameter(Parameter(
        "InstanceType",
        Type="String",
        Description="WebServer EC2 instance type",
        Default="m1.small",
        AllowedValues=[
            "t1.micro", "m1.small", "m1.medium", "m3.medium", "m1.large", "m1.xlarge",
            "m2.xlarge", "m2.2xlarge", "m2.4xlarge", "c1.medium", "c1.xlarge",
            "cc1.4xlarge", "cc2.8xlarge", "cg1.4xlarge"
        ],
        ConstraintDescription="must be a valid EC2 instance type.",
    ))

    webport_param = template.add_parameter(Parameter(
        "WebServerPort",
        Type="String",
        Default="8888",
        Description="TCP/IP port of the web server",
    ))
    # Define the security group for instances and ELB
    # note to reader: you need to use the name of the security group
    # not the ID. Remember this when your'e kicking it off from console
    SecurityGroup = template.add_parameter(Parameter(
        "SecurityGroup",
        Type="String",
        Description="Security group for api instances.",
    ))

    # Add the web server instances
    web_instances = []
    for name in ("Ec2Instance1", "Ec2Instance2"):
        instance = template.add_resource(ec2.Instance(
            name,
            SecurityGroups=[Ref(SecurityGroup)],
            KeyName=Ref(keyname_param),
            InstanceType=Ref("InstanceType"),
            ImageId=FindInMap("RegionMap", Ref("AWS::Region"), "AMI"),
            UserData=Base64(Ref(webport_param)),
        ))
        web_instances.append(instance)

    elasticLB = template.add_resource(elb.LoadBalancer(
        'ElasticLoadBalancer',
        SecurityGroups=[Ref(SecurityGroup)],
        AvailabilityZones=GetAZs(""),
        ConnectionDrainingPolicy=elb.ConnectionDrainingPolicy(
            Enabled=True,
            Timeout=300,
        ),
        CrossZone=True,
        Instances=[Ref(r) for r in web_instances],
        Listeners=[
            elb.Listener(
                LoadBalancerPort="80",
                InstancePort=Ref(webport_param),
                Protocol="HTTP",
            ),
        ],
        HealthCheck=elb.HealthCheck(
            Target=Join("", ["HTTP:", Ref(webport_param), "/"]),
            HealthyThreshold="3",
            UnhealthyThreshold="5",
            Interval="30",
            Timeout="5",
        )
    ))

    template.add_output(Output(
        "URL",
        Description="URL of the sample website",
        Value=Join("", ["http://", GetAtt(elasticLB, "DNSName")])
    ))

    print(template.to_json())


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

During the kick off from AWS console, using the name of the security group allows this obfuscated feature to work, however, when you add the same security group to the ELB instance you get this error from AWS Console

```
Invalid id: "nameofsecuritygrouphere" (expecting "sg-...") (Service: AmazonEC2; Status Code: 400; Error Code: InvalidGroupId.Malformed; Request ID: e8ddc4c7-1a92-4b82-a260-e53eb3c33b61)
```

issue_title: Glob warnings should be target-level, not individual-glob-level
issue_body:
https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/pull/5769 introduced warnings for globs that don't match anything.

However this check is at the individual glob level, not the target level. In other words, if you have multiple globs in a target, every one has to match something separately. 

For example, the default globs for python_tests is ('test_*.py', '*_test.py'), so that shops can pick whether they prefer test_foo.py or foo_test.py as their idiom. But now you'll get a warning on whichever one you didn't pick...

issue_title: Expose materialize_directory as an intrinsic function on scheduler
issue_body:
`fs::Store` already has a function to materialize a directory to disk: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/2512e9b4c7aed6c1f681b544e8cc94c66553fb9c/src/rust/engine/fs/src/store.rs#L410-L414

We should expose this as an intrinsic function on the scheduler, so that python code can call `scheduler.materialize_directories((DirectoryToMaterialize('/path/to/materialize', directory_digest),))` with a tuple of pairs of paths (`str`) and directory digests (`DirectoryDigest`), and which returns when these directories exist.

This will look pretty similar to https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/pull/5801 which introduced a new intrinsic function, except this PR shouldn't need to change the scheduler.

issue_title: golang tests run by pants omits resource files that can be necessary to run tests
issue_body:
When testing a golang project, if the tests depend upon resources in the directory, such as images (that would end in `.bmp`, or `.jpg`, for example), these tests fail when running `pants test target` because the modules files ending in `.go` are imported, but not other files.

For example:

In the following directory, you can see the following files:

```
$ ls -hl path/to/golang/source
total 208
-rw-r--r--  1 chad  staff   736B Nov 17 20:51 BUILD
-rw-r--r--  1 chad  staff   2.9K Nov 16 11:15 boxblur.go
-rw-r--r--  1 chad  staff    11K Oct 15 23:32 command.go
-rw-r--r--  1 chad  staff   5.5K Oct 15 23:32 command_test.go
-rw-r--r--  1 chad  staff   1.5K Oct 15 23:32 filtertype.go
-rw-r--r--  1 chad  staff    11K Oct 15 23:32 imgproc.go
-rw-r--r--  1 chad  staff   1.3K Nov 17 20:41 imgproc_test.go
-rw-r--r--  1 chad  staff   1.3K Nov 17 14:30 imgutil.go
-rw-r--r--  1 chad  staff   9.9K Nov 17 20:19 io.go
-rw-r--r--  1 chad  staff   2.2K Nov 17 20:41 io_test.go
-rw-r--r--  1 chad  staff   6.0K Oct 15 23:32 logo.go
-rw-r--r--  1 chad  staff   6.4K Nov 17 14:30 mediancutquantizer.go
-rw-r--r--  1 chad  staff   7.0K Oct 15 23:32 pixelmodifier.go
-rw-r--r--  1 chad  staff   730B Oct 15 23:32 pixelmodifier_test.go
-rw-r--r--  1 chad  staff   5.1K Oct 15 23:32 resample.go
drwxr-xr-x  3 chad  staff   102B Oct 20 10:25 test

where test is a directory that contains images that are used inside of the unit tests.

Looking at what is imported by pants the directory is missing the test directory, which causes the tests to fail.

 ```

```

issue_title: Implement CLI > target > config logic in the options system.
issue_body:
For some targets (like jvm_app) we want to have options at target level, in addition to options at CLI and config file level.

The precedence will be CLI > target > config.

Currently this logic is in pants/backend/jvm/tasks/bundle_create.py, but if we see more use cases for target level options, this logic should go to options system.

issue_title: Switch local process execution to use spawn_async not output_async
issue_body:
Here: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/26f1d4284d026c633653553bb36681b5cc8be7b5/src/rust/engine/process_execution/src/local.rs#L123 we execute a process, and only get its output when it finishes.

In order to support streaming of output, and in order to get partial output on timing out, it would be useful for us to call `spawn_async` instead of `output_async`, and to accumulate the process output ourselves, rather than relying on `output_async` to accumulate the output for us where we can't see it.

This will require configuring output streams on the `Command`, and changing the structure of how we consume the futures a little.

issue_title: Update Scala platform to fail on underspecified custom setup
issue_body:
When a user specifies a custom version of scala currently portions of ScalaPlatform fall back to defaults if the user doesn't create targets to override the defaults.

For example if the user specifies custom but does not create a //:scalac target pants will not complain but will compile with scala 2.10 which is the default. 

compiler_classpath and style_classpath should provide the user with a useful error message to indicate that scala tools are underspecified for a custom setup.

issue_title: Distribution has an inappropriate dependency on the jvm backend.
issue_body:
Distribution lives down in the core pants/java package because backends other than the jvm - python right now - need access to an Executor / Ivy to run java-based tools to non-java based work.  It would be inappropriate to distribute pants pared down for a pure python project and have this dependency suck in the jvm backend and all its unused code and tasks.

This is related to #2056 and was discovered in #2005 & #2054

issue_title: time() decorator is not thread-safe?
issue_body:
Good day,

I have a suspicion that time() decorator is not thread-safe in some way. Consider the following code:

```python
from prometheus_client import start_http_server, Summary
import sys
import time
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
from timeit import default_timer

S_TIME = Summary("timing_with_time", "Time spent processing request")
S_MANUAL = Summary("timing_manually", "Time spent processing request")

@S_TIME.time()
def process_request():
    """A dummy function that takes some time."""
    start = default_timer()
    time.sleep(1)
    S_MANUAL.observe(max(default_timer() - start, 0))
    
    
if __name__ == "__main__":
    start_http_server(8000)

    threads = int(sys.argv[1])
        
    pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=threads)
    for i in range(1000):
        pool.submit(process_request)
```

Running it as `python bug.py 1` produce the following metrics:
```
timing_with_time_count 12.0
timing_with_time_sum 12.013219268759713
timing_manually_count 12.0
timing_manually_sum 12.012461822945625
```
I.e. both _sum values are similar as expected. However if I increase the concurrency, then the metric that is
tracked with `time()` does NOT have its _sum increasing:

With `python bug.py 2`:
```
timing_with_time_count 10.0
timing_with_time_sum 5.004416585084982
timing_manually_count 10.0
timing_manually_sum 10.009211090975441
```
I.e. the count matches, but the sum of the first one is wrong! I did 10 requests each 1 second long, not 0.5 second long. The problem scales linearly. E.g. with `python bug.py 10`:
```
timing_with_time_count 60.0
timing_with_time_sum 6.0030247694812715
timing_manually_count 60.0
timing_manually_sum 60.06247438094579
```
I.e. _sum metric measured with `time()` decorator always thinks it's single threaded.

Python 3.6.5 on ubuntu 16.04 64-bit, P8s client version 0.2.0 (also happens with 0.1.1)

issue_title: data_structures.py : DidlResource - add to_dict method
issue_body:
When using get_queue.to_dict, it returns a dict that has a 'resources' element that is a DidlResource object -- which is not serializable in json.  Have a to_dict method on the DidlResource would make life easier, and then get_queue's to_dict could call to_dict on the DidlResource.

I'll submit a patch but I have a question first.  When a DidlResource property is None, should the to_dict method return {'property_foo': None, ...}  or just not include the key in the returned dict?  I would think that the first option is preferable as it requires simpler logic to deal with key=>None than deal with a missing key.    Let me know and I'll start on the patch.   (I'll open a second issue on the get_queue.to_dict calling to_dict on the DidlResource, if/when this patch is accepted, because that might have other ramifications.)

issue_title: Change policy to prefer support questions on google group over github
issue_body:
Hallo everyone.

For a while now we have been lagging a little behind with the issues and that sometimes means that important bug reports don't get the priority they should have and that PR's are not reviewed for too long.

One issue that I see is that we get both bug reports, enhancements (PR's) and support request here on github. Of those three items, the first 2 needs developers, but the support questions can often be handled by committed users.

I therefore suggest to change the description in various places (home page, Github front page readme and PyPI) to say that support questions are **preferably** reported via the google group and, if the google group is not available to a user, then reported on github.

If we don't mind requiring that people join the google group, we could also say that support requests go exclusively to the google group, but I think that is maybe a bit too restrictive for my taste.

What do you think about it?

Regards Kenneth

issue_title: Replace content app test fixtures
issue_body:
<!--
Instructions:
 * Fill out the sections below, replace …'s with information about your issue
 * Use the 'preview' function above this text box to verify formatting before submitting
-->

### Observed behavior
<!--
Description of the behavior that was observed, including screenshots or other references when applicable
-->

Right now in the content app, we load fixtures to use in our tests. These fixtures don't have meaningful names or attributes. Such as `"title": "c2"` or `"title": "c1c2"`. Also we have relations that won't normally happen in an actual content database, such as a `topic` node having an `audio` node parent. This makes it difficult to add new tests or add/modify fixtures. It was suggested to create a channel on studio, in which we could dump the data into a fixtures file to use for our tests. In this way, we are testing a true content database schema, with meaningful names and relations.

**Note: Since we currently test and have fixtures for each schema of an importable content database into kolibri, we would need to change the json fixtures for each of these schemas.**

-->

Easy to add new tests and reason about tests in PRs.

### DEV-facing consequences
<!--
Implications and real-world consequences for learners, coaches, admins, and other users of the application

issue_title: Sqlalchemy doesn't update nested elements in objects
issue_body:
Using sqlalchemy is not possible to update a nested element. 
# Example

Model:

```
>>> from crate.client.sqlalchemy.types import Object, ObjectArray
>>> from uuid import uuid4

>>> class Character(Base):
...     __tablename__ = 'characters'
...     id = sa.Column(sa.String, primary_key=True, default=gen_key)
...     name = sa.Column(sa.String)
...     quote = sa.Column(sa.String)
...     details = sa.Column(Object)
...     more_details = sa.Column(ObjectArray)
...     name_ft = sa.Column(sa.String)
...     quote_ft = sa.Column(sa.String)
```

We try to update a Character:

```
>>> char = session.query(Character).filter_by(name='Arthur Dent').one()
>>> char.details['gender'] = 'manly man'
>>> char.details['field_A']['sub_field_a'] = 'value_aaaa'
>>> session.commit()
>>> session.refresh(char)
```

SQLAlchemy generates the following query:

```
UPDATE characters SET details['gender'] = 'manly man' WHERE analysis.id = '955ce9c7-e85f-489d-b48c-bf6e216c10fe';
```

But char.details['field_A']['sub_field_a'] is not taken in account in the query. The expected query is:

```
UPDATE characters SET details['gender'] = 'manly man', details['field_A']['sub_field_a'] = 'value_aaaa' WHERE analysis.id = '955ce9c7-e85f-489d-b48c-bf6e216c10fe';
```
# Versions:

Python 2.7
Crate Data Python client 0.16.3
SQLalchemy 0.9.9
## <bountysource-plugin>

Want to back this issue? **[Post a bounty on it!](https://www.bountysource.com/issues/38319099-sqlalchemy-doesn-t-update-nested-elements-in-objects?utm_campaign=plugin&utm_content=tracker%2F505660&utm_medium=issues&utm_source=github)** We accept bounties via [Bountysource](https://www.bountysource.com/?utm_campaign=plugin&utm_content=tracker%2F505660&utm_medium=issues&utm_source=github).

```

reproduce:

```
mkdir app
cd app

foo

issue_title: UFO builds from lib.plist vs. contents.plist glyph lists
issue_body:
#### Build platforms/application versions:
fontmake v1.3.0 from PyPI
OS: OS X 10.12 + Ubuntu Trusty (Travis builds for both platforms)
UFO source spec v2 files

#### Request

- Consider UFO to ttf builds (and probably otf too...) from glyph lists in contents.plist rather than lib.plist

#### Issue

During a lib.plist cleanup, I removed the public.glyphOrder key/values in our source files and this caused our UFO to ttf fontmake build to fail with the following exception (Linux build shown):

```
INFO:fontmake.font_project:Saving TTF for Hack-Regular
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/opt/python/2.7.13/bin/fontmake", line 11, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
  File "/opt/python/2.7.13/lib/python2.7/site-packages/fontmake/__main__.py", line 195, in main
    ufo_paths, is_instance=args.pop('masters_as_instances'), **args)
  File "/opt/python/2.7.13/lib/python2.7/site-packages/fontmake/font_project.py", line 481, in run_from_ufos
    **kwargs)
  File "/opt/python/2.7.13/lib/python2.7/site-packages/fontmake/font_project.py", line 193, in build_ttfs
    self.save_otfs(ufos, ttf=True, **kwargs)
  File "/opt/python/2.7.13/lib/python2.7/site-packages/fontTools/misc/loggingTools.py", line 372, in wrapper
    return func(*args, **kwds)
  File "/opt/python/2.7.13/lib/python2.7/site-packages/fontmake/font_project.py", line 315, in save_otfs
    self.subset_otf_from_ufo(otf_path, ufo)
  File "/opt/python/2.7.13/lib/python2.7/site-packages/fontmake/font_project.py", line 329, in subset_otf_from_ufo
    for glyph_name in ufo.lib[PUBLIC_PREFIX + 'glyphOrder']
KeyError: u'public.glyphOrder'
Unable to build the Hack-Regular variant set.  Build canceled.
make: *** [build-with-dependencies] Error 1
```

My understanding is that lib.plist is an optional file in the UFO spec, though it appears to be mandatory for fontmake builds from UFO source files.  I wanted to see if you might consider converting to contents.plist (mandatory file in UFO spec) for the glyph lists necessary for builds from UFO source.

The rationale for this request on our end is that we are struggling with how to manage the editor dependent lib.plist data that is unnecessary in the remote git repository (we are trying to support any UFO source editing tool). Ideally (for our purposes), everyone keeps their editor dependent data locally where they need it and builds occur from data in mandatory files that do not rely on the editor in use by the developer who commits.  If we can add lib.plist to our .gitignore file, it eliminates the need for review of thousands of lines of source when an author modifies something like the glyph list order based upon downstream developer / editor preferences.

issue_title: Passing None doesn't work when it should
issue_body:
Some methods in the API take input arrays and allow None. As a matter of fact, None is the default value in some cases!
An example in NI-FGEN:

```
def create_arb_sequence(self, waveform_handles_array, loop_counts_array, sample_counts_array=None, marker_location_array=None):
```

`sample_counts_array` and `marker_location_array` are both optional (by passing None). The runtime should receive NULL for them, and know how to handle them. Right now this doesn't work because we naively try to get the len() of the array in order to allocate the ctypes object:

```
sample_counts_array_ctype = (visatype.ViInt32 * len(sample_counts_array))(*sample_counts_array)  # case 4
``` 

We'll need some extra logic to handle this right.

issue_title: Use batching/chunking timers
issue_body:
There are multiple places within Autobahn (at the WebSocket and at the WAMP level) where we use timers sticked to protocol instances.

When running massive numbers of connections, this can lead to significant CPU load.

Since these timers don't need to be precise, and hence we can conserve CPU by batching timeouts into buckets (and process timeout callbacks within a bucket in chunks).

The underlying functionality will be in `txaio`: https://github.com/crossbario/txaio/issues/66

Once that is done, we should use them here.

issue_title: How to contribute to documentation?
issue_body:
I'm wondering about how I can best contribute to documentation.

Running `python documentation/make.py` from my local fork produces an error, since it can't import music21.  However, I don't believe it should be necessary to build the documentation in order to edit the source files which the .html docs are being built from.

So for example, I wanted to correct the spelling error found here:  http://web.mit.edu/music21/doc/tutorials/examples.html

issue_title: Try pre-compiling numba code
issue_body:
I just learned that numba now supports ahead-of-time compilation, such that numba-generated code can be distributed in a package with only numpy as a dependency -- no numba or no LLVM for necessary for users.

I see two benefits (1) obviously, this makes it easier for non-conda users to enjoy numba-powered speedups and (2) I think it frees us to rely on bleeding-edge numba features.

issue_title: BUG: Particle added to track twice
issue_body:
I tried out the master branch for my regular analysis workflow. I got as far as running linking on some of my smaller movies, in this case ~54k particles for 19200 frames. It looks like they were failing because of an exception in `linking.utils.Point.add_to_track`: `trying to add a particle already in a track`. This occurred at some arbitrary point in each movie, after hundreds of frames.

This was regular linking, not `find_link`. Prediction and `adaptive_step` were in use. The data are generally messy and are no doubt a good place to find edge cases. Linking works fine with the v0.3 implementation.

I won't have time to investigate for a few more weeks, but I thought I would report this now since (a) it obviously needs a closer look before we release v0.4, and (b) @caspervdw might brilliantly think of the cause and save me some work 😉 .
